input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate its configuration to the modern MSTest format. Let me look at their files to understand the current setup.

data.content:
The `.testsettings` format is **legacy and ignored by MSTest v3+**. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent for your three settings:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property | `<TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Assembly resolution / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Code coverage data collector | `<DataCollector>` in `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces TestSettings TestTimeout property (30 000 ms) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / TestDirectory useLoadContext -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the TestSettings CodeCoverage data collector -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment config
2. A test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. A code coverage data collector

They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file, which is the modern format supported by MSTest v3+. The `.testsettings` format is legacy and no longer supported in modern MSTest.

Let me create a `.runsettings` file that captures the equivalent settings.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces TestSettings TestTimeout property (30 000 ms) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / TestDirectory useLoadContext -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the TestSettings CodeCoverage data collector -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
Created `local.runsettings`. To use it, either:

1. **CLI:** `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
2. **csproj (auto-use):** add to your `<PropertyGroup>`:
   ```xml
   <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
   ```

After confirming everything works, you can delete the old `local.testsettings` file — it has no effect with MSTest v3.
